segments.text: Moreover Job continued his discourse, and said:
As God lives, who has taken away my justice, And the Almighty, who has made my soul bitter,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: As long as my breath is in me, And the breath of God in my nostrils,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My lips will not speak wickedness, Nor my tongue utter deceit.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Far be it from me That I should say you are right; Till I die I will not put away my integrity from me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My righteousness I hold fast, and will not let it go; My heart shall not reproach me as long as I live.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: May my enemy be like the wicked, And he who rises up against me like the unrighteous.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For what is the hope of the hypocrite, Though he may gain much, If God takes away his life?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Will God hear his cry When trouble comes upon him?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Will he delight himself in the Almighty? Will he always call on God?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I will teach you about the hand of God; What is with the Almighty I will not conceal.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Surely all of you have seen it; Why then do you behave with complete nonsense?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: This is the portion of a wicked man with God, And the heritage of oppressors, received from the Almighty:
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If his children are multiplied, it is for the sword; And his offspring shall not be satisfied with bread.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Those who survive him shall be buried in death, And their widows shall not weep,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Though he heaps up silver like dust, And piles up clothing like clay—
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He may pile it up, but the just will wear it, And the innocent will divide the silver.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He builds his house like a moth, Like a booth which a watchman makes.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The rich man will lie down, But not be gathered up; He opens his eyes, And he is no more.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Terrors overtake him like a flood; A tempest steals him away in the night.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The east wind carries him away, and he is gone; It sweeps him out of his place.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: It hurls against him and does not spare; He flees desperately from its power.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Men shall clap their hands at him, And shall hiss him out of his place.
segments.speaker: Job
